about.description: I am a veterinarian, graduated from FMV-UTL, with over 10 years of experience in small companion animal clinics. I have a special interest in proximity care, focusing on welfare and quality of life, disease prevention, early diagnosis, and treatment. My mission is to provide home veterinary care, bringing comfort to animals and convenience to their owners.

blog.title: Pet Health Guide
blog.subtitle: Guidelines and information to better care for your pet

posts.title: Benefits of Home Veterinary Visits
posts.subtitle: Discover the advantages of caring for your pet in the comfort of home
items: Lower risk of contracting infectious diseases, especially during the initial vaccination period
Your pet does not get carsick or stressed by leaving its familiar environment
Owners save time on travel and waiting rooms — practical and convenient
More personalized and individual consultations, with greater attention to each pet
Ideal for elderly pets, those with reduced mobility, or those afraid to leave home
Humane euthanasia in terminal cases
The vet can more fully assess your pet's behavior and home environment

posts.title: Benefits of Vaccination
posts.subtitle: Why vaccination is essential for your pet's health
items: Protects your pet from several diseases, some of which can be fatal
Safety for your pet and the community: protects your pet, other animals, and public health
Routine vaccination contributes to a longer life and better quality of life
Prevention is always better and cheaper than treating serious illness

posts.title: Helping Your Cat Overcome Fear of the Vet
posts.subtitle: Tips to make veterinary visits less stressful for cats
items: Use a comfortable carrier and let your cat get used to it before the visit
Stay calm — cats sense their owner's anxiety
Consider home visits to eliminate the stress of travel
Reward good behavior with treats and affection
Avoid long fasting before the consultation unless necessary

posts.title: Exercising with Your Best Friend
posts.subtitle: Tips to keep your dog active and healthy
items: Adapted walks are the universal exercise for every dog
Mental stimulation also tires and trains — use interactive toys
Running is for athletic, well-conditioned dogs — always build up gradually
Tug-of-war builds confidence and muscle (teach the 'drop' and 'wait' commands)
Short training sessions are tiring (sit, lie down, paw for 5–10 minutes with positive reinforcement)
The greatest sign of love is respecting your dog's limits — watch for signs of fatigue

posts.title: Obesity in Pets
posts.subtitle: How to prevent and tackle excess weight in pets
items: Regular body condition assessment with a vet is essential
Control food access — avoid free feeding
Promote frequent physical activity adapted to your pet
Technology like smart collars can help with monitoring
Choose healthy treats, such as appropriate fruits and vegetables
Obesity can lead to diabetes, joint problems, and a shorter life expectancy

posts.title: Coping with the Loss of a Pet
posts.subtitle: Emotional support for owners who have lost their companions
items: Grief for the loss of a pet is real and should be respected
Allow yourself to feel all emotions — sadness, anger, and guilt are normal
Share memories and photos with family and friends who understand
Consider creating a tribute — a photo album, planting a tree
Do not rush into getting another pet — respect your grieving time
Seek professional support if you struggle to cope with the loss
